div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
independent path:
(no gotoSpace dispatched →
no chain to clear)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
sync-group path:
(chain finished →
verify, then clear)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: yes

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p: no

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(207, 230, 240) !important; text-align: center; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; width: 200px;
text-align: left;

span.div:
Key concepts — read this first
• Space — a macOS virtual desktop on a single display. Each display has its own list.
• Sync group — a list of displays that should track each other. When any one switches Space, the others follow.
• Trigger display — the display the user actually switched. Target display — another display in the same sync group that we drive to match.
• Spaces watcher = hs.spaces.watcher. Fires on Space changes — primary entry path.
• Screen watcher = hs.screen.watcher. (new in v3) Fires on display layout changes (plug/unplug, lid open/close, rearrange) — second entry path.
• gotoSpace = hs.spaces.gotoSpace. Dispatch only — v3 verifies via polling rather than waiting an arbitrary time.
• lastActiveSpaces — snapshot of prior per-display Space state. v3 writes it per target after each poll-verify, using the observed post-poll value (Q1; F-010 §6.2). Echoes naturally diff to no-change.
• syncInProgress — boolean. true during the chain's polling window. v3 window ≈ actual chain duration; v0.2 window was chain + debounce padding (~1.7s for 4 displays).
• pollTimeout — upper bound on waiting for a single gotoSpace to verify. ~2 s (F-010 §6.1). Replaces fixed switchDelay.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
User switches a Space.
Via Mission Control gesture, ⌃→/⌃←,
a Dock click, or other macOS UI.

div.style: color: rgb(238, 238, 238) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
macOS commits the switch.
The chosen Space is now active on that display.

div.style: color: rgb(238, 238, 238) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Spaces watcher fires our callback.
hs.spaces.watcher invokes our Lua callback —
the signal that a Space somewhere changed.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is SpacesSync enabled?
(false after the user toggles off via the
toggle hotkey or :stop)

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Return — SpacesSync is off.
How we got here: user pressed the toggle hotkey
(default ⌃⌥⌘Y), or called spoon.SpacesSync:stop()
from init.lua, the Hammerspoon console, or hs -c.
Why we still need this guard: when :stop runs,
macOS may already be partway through calling our
watcher function. You can't kill an in-flight call —
so this guard makes that one straggler bail out
instead of finishing a sync the user just stopped.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Already running a sync chain?
syncInProgress == true while we are dispatching
and polling for verification of gotoSpace calls.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Drop the event.
Whole point of syncInProgress: swallow our own
gotoSpace echoes that fire while we're still polling.
Window length in v3 = sum of per-target poll times.
(Targets = sync-group size − 1; trigger excluded.)
Typical case: numTargets × ~750 ms (F-010 mean) — for
a 4-display group (3 targets), ~2.3 s typical (about 35% longer
than v0.2's 1.7 s ceiling, but driven by actual macOS settle
rather than an arbitrary clock — slightly slower in exchange
for not silently dropping calls when macOS is slow).
Worst case: numTargets × pollTimeout (2 s) = 6 s for 4 displays
if every dispatch hits its timeout — strictly worse than v0.2.
The trade is reliability over fixed-bound: typical case ≈ v0.2;
pathological case slower; correctness much better.
Real user swipes inside this window are still dropped.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Diff to find what changed.
Compare hs.spaces.activeSpaces against lastActiveSpaces
to find which display(s) just switched.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Did anything actually change?
Did at least one display's active Space change
since the last snapshot?
Note: if multiple displays changed in the same
watcher fire, only the FIRST one found becomes the trigger.
The others are folded into the baseline silently.
(See OmniFocus task on multi-changed handling.)

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
No actual Space change — exit.
The watcher fires on more than real Space switches:
Mission Control opening, animation settling,
Stage Manager events. The diff confirms nothing
actually changed — refresh the snapshot defensively
and exit. v3 also reaches this from gotoSpace echoes
because the per-target observed-value writes already
updated the baseline to match macOS's confirmed state.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Find the trigger's sync group.
Map the trigger display's UUID → position number,
then scan syncGroups for a group containing that position.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Trigger in a sync group?
Is the trigger display a member of any group?

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Independent display — nothing to sync.
Trigger is not in any sync group.
We dispatch zero gotoSpace calls on this path,
so there are no echoes to worry about — which is
why we don't need syncInProgress here.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Set syncInProgress = true; bump chainGeneration. (council mitigation)
From now until the chain's verifier completes,
spaces-watcher fires are dropped. The chainGeneration
integer is captured by every poll/dispatch closure;
BAIL_CHAIN, :stop, and the watchdog all bump it,
so any in-flight closure can detect 'I was bailed'
and exit before doing further damage.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Compute the expected end state. (new in v3)
Snapshot hs.spaces.activeSpaces; this is the starting world.
For each target in the sync group, replace its entry with
the Space ID at the trigger's index on that target.
The result is a per-display UUID → Space ID map describing
exactly what we want the world to look like when the chain finishes.
This single map drives both the per-target dispatches during
the chain AND the verification at chain end — one source of truth.
(Note: per Q1 the per-target lastActiveSpaces writes use the
OBSERVED post-poll value, not the value from this map.)

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Iterate target displays.
Targets = sync-group members minus the trigger.
Start at i = 1.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Dispatch hs.spaces.gotoSpace.
Look up the target's expected Space ID from
expectedEndState[targetUUID] (computed at chain start),
then call gotoSpace. macOS animates the switch.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Poll for verification. (replaces switchDelay in v3)
Every ~30 ms, re-read hs.spaces.activeSpaceOnScreen(target).
Continue when it matches the expected Space ID,
OR pollTimeout (~2 s) elapses — whichever comes first.
Each poll iteration also re-checks state.enabled and
chainGeneration; bails if either changed.
Calibrated via F-010 polling spike: Model B holds —
activeSpaceOnScreen flips at end of MC animation,
so polling alone is a sufficient ready signal.
Mean flip latency 753 ms; pollTimeout 2 s covers slow settle.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Did the target reach the expected Space?
If yes — verified, move on.
If no (poll timeout) — macOS dropped the dispatch
or settle was unexpectedly slow.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Log dispatch drop.
Warning level: 'target X: gotoSpace did not land
within pollTimeout.' Don't block the chain —
continue to OBSERVED_WRITE so the baseline reflects
macOS's actual stuck state, not a lie.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Write observed value to baseline. (Q1 resolution, new in v3)
Read activeSpaceOnScreen(target) once more
and set state.lastActiveSpaces[targetUUID] = observed.
On success: observed == expected (the dispatch landed).
On poll timeout: observed == macOS's actual stuck state
(end-of-chain verifier will surface this as drift).
Either way, the baseline matches reality — echoes that
arrive after this point diff to no-change naturally;
no debounce window needed. (Per F-010 §6.2: cheaper
than pre-dispatch expected-value writes, and can't poison
the baseline if macOS dropped the call.)

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
More targets?
Any target displays remaining in the sync group?
Note: no inter-dispatch wait. F-010 confirmed Model B
(160/160 dispatches at gap=0); polling is sufficient.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Verify world matches expected end state. (scoped to sync-group path)
Read actual = hs.spaces.activeSpaces.
Diff against expectedEndState (computed at chain start).
The diff covers EVERY display in expectedEndState —
sync-group members AND independents AND the trigger.
Per-mismatch severity (the diff alone tells you the role):
• Sync-group display at wrong Space → ERROR (sync genuinely failed)
• Trigger at wrong Space → ERROR (user moved it mid-chain;
expectedEndState is now stale; we did NOT remediate)
• Independent display at wrong Space → INFO (user swiped it
during our chain; we dropped the signal; surfaced for awareness)
• Display vanished/appeared → ERROR (reconfig race)
Detection ≠ remediation. We log; we do NOT auto-retry.
Then refresh state.lastActiveSpaces = actual so the next
watcher fire diffs against truth. User re-swipes if they care.
Scope: runs ONLY on the sync-group path. NO_CHANGE just
confirmed nothing changed; independent path didn't dispatch
anything; reconfig path already rebuilt state from scratch.
Runs BEFORE CLEAR so the gate stays closed during verify.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Show popup on trigger display.
The active Space index with names from the
workspace name store. Auto-dismisses after popupDuration.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Clear syncInProgress — re-arm. (simplified in v3)
The last target verified (or timed out) and the
end-of-chain verifier confirmed world matches expected.
Set syncInProgress = false.
No debounce wait — per-target observed-value writes
already absorbed any echoes that may still arrive.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Update lastActiveSpaces baseline.
Used by the independent-display path:
set lastActiveSpaces = currentSpaces
so the next watcher fire diffs against
the post-switch state.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Watcher armed.
Ready for the next event from either watcher.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Display layout changes.
Monitor plugged/unplugged, lid open/close,
dock/undock, displays rearranged in System Settings,
resolution change.

div.style: color: rgb(238, 238, 238) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Screen watcher fires our callback. (new in v3)
hs.screen.watcher invokes our Lua callback.
Distinct from spaces-watcher — this fires on layout change,
not on Space change.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Coalesce burst. (council fix, new in v3)
hs.screen.watcher fires 2-5× per real reconfig event
as macOS settles geometry. Schedule the rebuild via
hs.timer.doAfter(~250 ms); cancel and re-arm on
subsequent fires within the window. One rebuild per
real reconfig instead of one per fire.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Is SpacesSync enabled?
If not, return — same logic as the swipe path.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Was a sync chain in flight?
If yes, its targets may have just disappeared
or shifted positions — we need to abort cleanly.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Abort the in-flight chain.
Bump chainGeneration so any captured closure bails on next tick.
Set syncInProgress = false.
Stop ALL chain-owned timers (pollTimer + pendingSyncTimer +
watchdog). Track them in a single chainTimers list so this
is one cleanup call, not per-timer.
Refresh state.lastActiveSpaces = hs.spaces.activeSpaces.
(Without this, a partial chain leaves Frankenstein baseline:
some targets have post-poll observed values, others have
pre-chain values — the next watcher fire would diff wrong.)
Log at info: 'Reconfig during chain — aborting.'

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Rebuild position map.
Re-read hs.screen.allScreens, sort by frame x then y,
reassign position numbers in reading order.
Update positionToUUID, uuidToPosition, totalScreens.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Refresh lastActiveSpaces.
The set of UUIDs has likely changed.
Re-snapshot from current state so the next
spaces-watcher fire has a valid baseline to diff against.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Validate syncGroups against new layout.
For each position N referenced in syncGroups,
check N ≤ totalScreens. If any reference is now
out of range, log a warning so the user knows
their config refers to a display that's not connected.

div.style: color: rgb(255, 255, 255) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Show status HUD on main display.
'SpacesSync: display layout changed.'
The user knows we noticed and adapted.
Without this, reconfig would be silent.

div.style: color: rgb(240, 232, 200) !important; text-align: center; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; width: 200px;
text-align: left;

span.div:
Design intent — v3 verify-based redesign
The goal: only human-initiated swipes trigger sync. Our gotoSpace echoes must not cause secondary syncs. Plus: the system should fail loudly when something is genuinely wrong.
How v3 achieves it:
• Per-target verification via polling. Each gotoSpace is followed by a poll loop that waits for hs.spaces.activeSpaceOnScreen(target) to match the expected Space ID, with a pollTimeout fallback. Replaces the fragile fixed switchDelay.
• Per-target observed-value baseline writes. After each poll-verify, we read activeSpaceOnScreen one more time and write the OBSERVED value into lastActiveSpaces (Q1 resolution; F-010 §6.2). Echoes diff to no-change naturally — no debounce window needed. The observed-value formulation cannot poison the baseline if macOS drops a dispatch (the baseline reflects reality, not optimism).
• syncInProgress gate, narrow. Suppresses watcher fires during the in-flight polling window. Clears immediately after the end-of-chain verifier — no debounce padding.
• Screen watcher as second entry path. hs.screen.watcher fires on display layout changes; we rebuild the position map, refresh the baseline, validate the config, and show a status HUD. No more silent breakage on monitor unplug.
• Hard block on missing Accessibility at :start. hs.spaces.gotoSpace and hs.eventtap both require Accessibility permission. If revoked or never granted, :start fails loudly with an alert; no silent broken state.
What v3 drops vs. v0.2:
• ⊖ Fixed switchDelay (0.3 s × N) → variable polling, bounded by pollTimeout.
• ⊖ debounceSeconds (0.8 s) → eliminated entirely. Per-target observed-value writes absorb echoes by keeping lastActiveSpaces matched to macOS truth.
• ⊖ Fixed ~1.7 s ignore window for 4 monitors → typical case ~2.3 s (numTargets × ~750 ms), driven by real settle not arbitrary clock. Worst case (every dispatch times out): numTargets × pollTimeout = 6 s for 4 displays. Trade-off: typical ≈ v0.2; pathological worse; correctness much better.
• ⊖ Static position map → actively maintained via screen watcher.
• ⊖ Silent Accessibility failure → loud hard block.
What v3 still accepts:
• ⚠ Real user swipes inside the chain polling window are still dropped — polling doesn't queue them. Window typical ≈ v0.2's; pathological worse (see DROP node math). Bounded by actual macOS settle, not an arbitrary ceiling. User re-swipes if needed.
• ⚠ If macOS drops a gotoSpace and we can't verify within pollTimeout, we log warning and continue. No automatic retry. User re-swipes; that's the recovery.
Failure modes that loudly tell the user something is wrong:
• ✅ :start with Accessibility revoked → alert + abort
• ✅ syncInProgress stuck → watchdog clears it, logs at error level
• ✅ gotoSpace verification timeout → warning log per affected target
• ✅ Display reconfig → status HUD + log
Open follow-up tasks (filed in OmniFocus, not blocking):
• Per-display 'currently driving' set to replace global syncInProgress (multi-group correctness)
• Stage Manager / fullscreen Spaces support
• Multi-changed-display handling
• macOS 16 (Tahoe) re-verification — F-010 is macOS-15-specific
• Reduce Motion regime — F-010 didn't measure with animations off
• Heavy-load regime — F-010 ran on quiet system

svg.text: SpacesSync — runtime sync workflow (ROADMAP v3 — verify-based, no arbitrary waits)
